entries.title: Where am I in the process? Match your dashboard state. Eight possible states. Find yours below — and exactly what to do next.
entries.body_text: Open search.cvc.edu , log in, and look at the Status on your Class Schedule (or Application Details). Match what you see to one of the eight below. The state name on your dashboard tells you exactly which step the system is on — and which one (if any) needs your action. The 5 happy-path states (top track in the diagram) 1 Created You started the AER form. Nothing automated yet — the system just opened a record. What to do: finish the AER. If you abandoned it, go back and complete it. 2 Eligible & Approved CVC's automated checks passed (you're enrolled at your HC, GPA is fine, no holds, etc.). The system is now handing the registration to the Teaching College. What to do: nothing — wait. This usually advances within minutes. Full description. 3 Enrolled (at TC SIS) The Teaching College has written your record into their system. The class registration step is next. What to do: nothing — usually advances within hours, sometimes a day. If stuck more than 2 days, see step 4 (likely a Pending state). 4 Pending TC Review (one of three flavors) The TC needs to do one more thing before registration finalizes. The state name on your dashboard tells you which: Pending Person Match — the TC needs to confirm your CCCID maps to a record on their side. Common at PeopleSoft colleges. Full description. Registered & Pending Validation — registration is in their SIS, awaiting one final validation step (often residency confirmation). Full description. Prerequisite Pending Review — the TC's A&R is reviewing your transcript. 3–5 business days for CCC prereqs. Full description. What to do: wait. Use the date checker to see when to follow up. Identify a backup section in case the class fills during your wait — see the seat-reservation gap entry . 5 Validated & Registered ✓ You're in. The TC has confirmed your registration on their side. What to do: pay any fees through the dashboard, then check your Home College's Canvas in 24–48 hours for the class to appear. Full description. The 3 off-ramp states (red boxes in the diagram) If your dashboard shows one of these, the registration didn't complete. The off-ramp tells you why. ↓ Ineligible The system stopped the registration before reaching the TC because of a CVC-side check. Four common causes: (a) you're a new student at your HC who hasn't registered for any HC class yet — see "I'm a new student" ; (b) Canvas at your HC doesn't show you as currently provisioned (often Merced-specific); (c) your HC GPA is below 2.0; (d) holds at your HC. Click "Details" on the failed row to see the specific reason. ↓ Registration Failed The TC accepted your application but couldn't complete the class registration. Most common cause: section filled while you were in the queue. Other causes: prereq decision was "not met," unit overload ( "Petition is required" ), or a TC-side record lock. The Details panel names the reason verbatim. ↓ Drop Failed A drop attempt didn't complete. Most common cause: a hold on your TC account (financial). Less commonly: past the drop deadline. Full description. One state worth a separate flag — if your dashboard says "Pending" but you got a confirmation email saying you're registered, you ARE registered. The dashboard sometimes lags by hours. Trust the email. Full description. Don't see your state listed above? The Status field uses these exact names. If yours is different, take a screenshot and use the ticket builder to email support — that lets us look at the underlying record directly.

entries.title: It's after-hours or a weekend and a deadline is approaching Plan B when support is closed
entries.body_text: CVC support staffs business hours, Monday through Friday. If you're reading this at midnight on Saturday with a Tuesday drop deadline, here's the realistic plan: Document everything right now. Screenshot the error, the timestamp, the dashboard state. Note exactly what you tried. This timestamped record is your strongest protection if something goes wrong. Email CVC support anyway. Use subject line "URGENT — DEADLINE [exact date and time]". Even though no one reads it until Monday, the timestamp on your email proves you reached out before the deadline. Use the ticket builder . Email the Teaching College's Admissions & Records office in parallel. Find their email at cvc.edu/students/refunds or on the college's website. Tell them: "I attempted to drop CRN [N] on [date/time] and the system showed [error]. I want this on record before the [date] deadline." That's documented intent at the TC, separate from CVC. Don't keep retrying. Repeated drop attempts create duplicate records that slow Monday's resolution. One careful try, screenshot, then stop. If the deadline truly passes despite your timely effort, both CVC and the Teaching College can advocate for a late-drop petition with that paper trail. The system isn't designed to punish students for our errors — but the documentation is what makes advocacy possible. Important — the deadline will not be extended just because support is closed. Your documentation creates the case for a retroactive exception, not an automatic one. Better to have the trail than not.

entries.title: My drop deadline is today or tomorrow What to do if drop fails or you're stuck
entries.body_text: Try the drop one time. Don't retry repeatedly — that doesn't help, and it creates duplicate records that slow resolution. Take a screenshot of any error. The exact error text determines what kind of fix is needed. Email support immediately with subject "URGENT — DROP DEADLINE [date]" and your CCCID, course/CRN, and screenshot. Use the ticket builder to format it correctly. Email the Teaching College's Admissions & Records office in parallel. Your email is documented intent to drop before the deadline. If anything goes wrong on our end, that record protects you. Find the A&R email at cvc.edu/students/refunds . Note. Drop deadlines are per-section, not per-term. Look up your specific date on the Teaching College's deadline tool — don't assume the standard semester schedule.

entries.title: My card was charged but I'm not enrolled Almost always the missing Confirm Payment click
entries.body_text: Log in to search.cvc.edu . Look at your Class Schedule. Does it say "Pay Now" or "Enrolled & Paid"? If it says "Pay Now": the system never received your payment confirmation. The fix is on our side; we can reconcile it. Email support with your CCCID, course, payment date/time, last 4 of card, and amount. If it says "Enrolled & Paid": you're fine — the dashboard caught up. Done. Don't pay again. A second payment goes to the same place and creates a refund headache, not enrollment. Why this happens. Students close the browser after the bank confirms the charge — but before clicking the Confirm Payment button on the page CVC redirects you to. Always click Confirm Payment.

entries.title: Class begins in 48 hours and I'm still Pending Exact steps before you panic-email
entries.body_text: Check your CVC inbox at search.cvc.edu after login. The Teaching College may have requested a document. If they did, you have to respond before they can finish. Confirm the status hasn't changed. "Pending Person Match" and "Prerequisite Pending Review" both look stuck — but they mean the Teaching College is actively reviewing you. They are not denied. Email support with the words "URGENT — CLASS START [date]" in the subject line, plus the information from the ticket builder below . Replies on time-sensitive tickets are typically same-day. While you wait, identify a backup section at search.cvc.edu . If the original Teaching College can't move fast enough, you may need to register for a different open section.

entries.title: Eligible and Approved normal — system green-lit you
entries.body_text: What it means CVC's eligibility check passed. The system has confirmed you meet the requirements (18+, 2.0 GPA, currently enrolled at HC, no holds, in-state address) and routed your request to the Teaching College. Whose ball The Teaching College's. They're processing your registration into their SIS. Realistic timeline Usually moves to "Registered & Pending Validation" then "Validated & Registered" within 1–3 business days. "Skipped home and teaching approvals"? If you see this note, it means a counselor cleared you via multiple-measures placement and the prereq exchange flow was bypassed. Normal — not a problem. No action needed — you're moving through the flow correctly. Use the date checker if you're impatient.

entries.title: Registered & Pending Validation wait — almost done
entries.body_text: What it means The Teaching College has registered you in their SIS. They're now running their final validation step (often a person-match or eligibility re-check on their side). Whose ball The Teaching College's, but typically automated and quick. Realistic timeline 1–2 business days then transitions to "Validated & Registered." When to email support After 3 business days, OR if class starts within 48 hours. No action needed yet — this is the second-to-last step.

entries.title: Validated & Registered all good — you're done
entries.body_text: What it means You're enrolled. Both CVC and the Teaching College agree you're in the class. This is the success state — the goal of the registration flow. Whose ball Yours. Pay if you haven't (see payment trap ) and check Canvas in 24–48 hours. What's next Pay fees → confirm "Enrolled & Paid" → wait for Canvas access → take the class. Grades flow back to your Home College after the term ends (4–6 weeks). Common confusion Some students see "Validated & Registered" but their dashboard still says "Pay Now." That's correct — you're registered but haven't paid yet. Two different states. No action needed — this is the outcome you wanted. Make sure you've paid (see §iii payment entries if your status is unclear).
